---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Kritika"
date: "2026-03-04"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r rt_trial_learning_plot, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# -----------------------------
# Folder paths
# -----------------------------

lab_single_path <- "/home/kritika/MTech/Sem4/BRSM/Project/Attention Task Validation/data_brsm/single/lab"
lab_multiple_path <- "/home/kritika/MTech/Sem4/BRSM/Project/Attention Task Validation/data_brsm/multiple/lab"

# -----------------------------
# Get all CSV files
# -----------------------------

single_files <- list.files(lab_single_path, pattern="*.csv", full.names=TRUE)
multiple_files <- list.files(lab_multiple_path, pattern="*.csv", full.names=TRUE)

# -----------------------------
# Function to safely read files
# -----------------------------

read_safe <- function(file){

  df <- read.csv(file,
                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                 colClasses = "character")

  # remove rows with missing mouse time
  df <- df[!is.na(df$mouse.time),]

  # extract first timestamp from mouse.time
  df$IRT <- as.numeric(str_extract(df$mouse.time, "[0-9.]+")) * 1000

  # create trial number
  df$trial <- 1:nrow(df)

  return(df)
}

# -----------------------------
# Load all participant files
# -----------------------------

lab_single <- bind_rows(lapply(single_files, read_safe))
lab_multiple <- bind_rows(lapply(multiple_files, read_safe))

lab_single$Condition <- "Single Target"
lab_multiple$Condition <- "Multiple Target"

lab_data <- bind_rows(lab_single, lab_multiple)

# -----------------------------
# Remove extreme outliers (optional but recommended)
# -----------------------------

lab_data <- lab_data %>%
  filter(IRT > 200 & IRT < 5000)

# -----------------------------
# Compute median RT per trial
# -----------------------------

trial_summary <- lab_data %>%
  group_by(Condition, trial) %>%
  summarise(
    median_RT = median(IRT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# -----------------------------
# Plot Learning / Fatigue Curve
# -----------------------------

ggplot(trial_summary, aes(x = trial, y = median_RT, color = Condition)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Reaction Time vs Trial Number (Learning Effect)",
    x = "Trial Number",
    y = "Median Initial Response Time (ms)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )


```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
